reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff (May 15, 2025), Serbia has recently stabilized its government through the appointment of a new Prime Minister, Djuro Macut, on April 16, 2025. This move was explicitly described by President Vučić and domestic media as a means to avoid immediate snap elections following the resignation of the previous PM amid protests. Although student protests continued into May 2025, the ruling party officially rejected calls for snap elections on May 6, 2025, stating that the new cabinet is intended to serve until the regular 2027 cycle. Given the recent investment in a new government and the official rejection of early polls just weeks before the July 1 deadline, an announcement of a general election is highly unlikely.
